what_it_does: Documents the Vi UI kit: describes Vi as ConceptV's internal AI framework that reads project-connected entities (Brand Kit, Master Info, Files, Analytics), finds missing fields, asks the user for gaps, and consolidates a finished asset (brochure, landing page, hub). Specifies the canonical 'Vi Workspace' three-pane layout, the Vi brand mark, the component file roster, and how the demo behaves.
observations: Defines a three-pane layout: left Conversation (chat where Vi shows its work), centre Task tree (the three-layer agent architecture, live), right Output preview (the asset built in real time).
Describes Vi's three-layer agent architecture: Layer 1 plans, Layer 2 spawns clones that analyse and break down sub-tasks, Layer 3 executes and saves to file.
References two illustration assets the layout mirrors: assets/illustrations/vi_brochure_flow.png and assets/illustrations/vi_layers.jpg.
Specifies Vi's brand mark as a diamond with line-fill used as the avatar, and a 'Vi' wordmark with a gold (#E0C010) subscript 'i'.
Lists six components with one-line roles: ViMark.jsx, ViStatusPill.jsx, ChatPanel.jsx, TaskTree.jsx, OutputPreview.jsx, App.jsx.
Describes the demo (index.html) opening mid-task building a 'Tower East' brochure, with Continue and Reset controls.
standouts: The three-layer agent architecture (plan / spawn-clones / execute-and-save) is named explicitly as the centre pane's content.
Vi reads connected entities and fills/flags gaps as the core product loop, surfaced live in the conversation pane.
conventions: Uses a markdown table to map panes to content and another to map component files to roles.
Inline HTML (<sub style=...>) used inside markdown to render the gold subscript wordmark.
notes: The spec/orientation doc for the Vi UI kit — read it to understand the three-pane Workspace, the three-layer agent visualisation, and which file does what.
questions: Are vi_brochure_flow.png and vi_layers.jpg present at the referenced asset paths?
ChatPanel.jsx and OutputPreview.jsx are listed here but not in this batch — do they exist as described?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is the UI kit README and it describes the kit's layout, brand, components, and demo.
apparent_themes: Entity-driven asset generation (read connected entities -> fill -> flag gaps -> consolidate)
Live visibility of agent work (Vi shows its reasoning/steps)
Three-layer agent architecture as a first-class UI surface
intention_signals: Tries to establish a single canonical screen ('Vi Workspace') that makes Vi's hidden agent work visible — conversation, agent task tree, and output preview side by side — so the AI's process is legible to the user.
novelty: Surfacing a multi-layer agent execution tree as a live centre pane alongside chat and output preview is a distinctive product framing for an AI asset-builder.
contribution: Contributes the conceptual spec and vocabulary for the Vi product face: the three-pane Workspace, the entity-read/gap-fill/consolidate loop, the three-layer agent model, and the diamond brand mark.
summary_for_embedding: README for the ConceptV Vi UI kit describing a three-pane Vi Workspace (conversation, live three-layer agent task tree, output preview) for an AI that reads project entities, fills gaps, and consolidates assets like brochures.
intention_for_embedding: To define and orient agents to the canonical Vi Workspace interface and its entity-driven, three-layer-agent asset-building loop.
